compiled_state.title: [verify-review-diff.sh]: line 55 compares full 64-char filename hash to 8-char truncated current hash -> verify always fails for full-hash diff filenames
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
### 2. Incident Overview

* **Scenario Type:** Sub-Agent Blocker

#### Expected Behavior

`verify-review-diff.sh` line 55 should compare compatible hash representations. The hash extracted from the filename (line 42 via sed) is the full 64-char SHA256. The current hash (line 52) is truncated to 8 chars via `${current_hash:0:8}`. These will never be equal.

#### Actual Behavior

The comparison on line 55:
```bash
if [ "$file_hash" = "$current_hash_short" ]; then
```
compares `f4ca4f4ca3942df0078c6a021f08dbc64cbb2d681d95df55ff4a5a1285519327` (64 chars from filename) to `f4ca4f4c` (8 chars truncated). Result: always `DIFF_VALID: no`.

Diff file names created by the review workflow contain the full 64-char hash (e.g., `review-diff-f4ca4f4ca394...txt`). The sed extraction on line 42 captures all 8+ hex characters — the full 64-char hash. But `current_hash_short` is only 8 chars.

#### Workaround Applied

Created a copy of the diff file with only the first 8 chars as the filename hash: `review-diff-f4ca4f4c.txt`. The sed pattern `\{8,\}` captures exactly 8 chars from this filename, allowing the comparison to succeed.

#### Fix

Line 55 should either:
```bash
# Compare both truncated:
if [ "${file_hash:0:8}" = "$current_hash_short" ]; then
# OR compare both full:
if [ "$file_hash" = "$current_hash" ]; then
```

#### Why Standard/Light Reviewers Sometimes Pass Despite This Bug

The standard and light reviewers appear to read the human-readable output (`DIFF_VALID: no (file: f4ca4f4c..., current: f4ca4f4c)`) and detect that the first 8 chars match, proceeding anyway. The test quality reviewer strictly follows the exit code and stops on non-zero.

comments.body: Classification: mechanical — comparison of incompatible hash lengths (64-char filename hash vs 8-char truncated current hash). Fix: truncate file_hash to 8 chars before comparison.
comments.author: Test
